feed.title: SAP Community - Data and Analytics
feed.subtitle: Data and Analytics blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Generate Custom Legal Certificates in SAP DRC
entry.content:
The purpose of withholding tax is to ensure that the government receives tax revenue in a timely manner and to facilitate tax compliance. It is commonly applied to various types of income such as wages, salaries, interest, dividends, royalties, and payments to contractors or freelancers. The rates of withholding tax and the types of income subject to withholding tax vary depending on the tax laws of each jurisdiction.

While SAP provides standard reports for reporting withholding tax, custom requirements may arise for specific needs. This blog focuses on the development of a custom certificate to address such requirements. Developing a custom certificate requires knowledge in DRC extensibility and Adobe Form development.

In this scenario, a custom Adobe form is being created within the system, extending the Thailand 50 BIS certificate.

Go to t-code SFP to create the Adobe Form. The form can be developed using the default interface SOMU_DEFAULT_INTERFACE, with XFA2 chosen as the layout technology.

Once the form layout is created, save and activate it.
Then, navigate to Define Compliance Application and initiate the creation of a new report definition. For this example, an existing report definition is duplicated. Provide a name for the new report definition and specify a package for its storage.

Create custom report definition

 

Save it in local package

After creating a new report definition, Add a new document according to the specified requirements. In this instance, a PDF document is presented as an example.

Add new legal document

 

Create it as a PDF

Provide details for the document such as the document name, file name, code page, etc.

Document Details

Select a schema for this document, which defines the format and structure of the document. Various options for creating a schema are available, including using XSD, JSON, or ABAP dictionary. For this particular example, the schema is generated using the ABAP dictionary. Alternatively, an existing schema can also be utilized.

Create Schema

 

Enter the Custom Form Details

After finalizing the schema, proceed to the Mapping section. The structure of the document, based on the schema, will be displayed, allowing for mapping according to the requirements. Data can be mapped from various sources such as CDS, tables, class methods, or system fields.

Perform Mapping

Save and Activate the report definition.

Save and Activate

Now the schema needs to be downloaded in order to perform mapping in the Adobe form. Select the "Export Schema" option and then choose "Export as Simplified XSD". Ensure that the report definition is in editable mode before attempting to download the schema.

Download XSD as shown

The layout for the Adobe form can be created using the Adobe LiveCycle Designer (ALD). Develop a custom layout, and if there's a requirement for a fixed company logo, watermark, or signature, it can be directly incorporated. For dynamic company logo, watermark, or signature needs, include an image field in the Adobe Form. The value of this field can be manipulated during form generation

Create Custom Layout

In this example, an already developed Thailand 50 BIS form has been downloaded. It contains existing bindings. To remove these bindings, navigate to the data view, right-click, and select "Delete Data Connection". During deletion, you have the option to retain or delete the existing bindings. In this instance, the existing bindings are kept intact while mapping new fields in the form.

Delete Data Connection

 

Do not remove existing binding

To establish a new data connection, right-click in the Data View and select "New Data Connection". Upload the previously downloaded schema from the steps above and utilize it for mapping the new fields.

Create New Data Connection

 

Choose XML schema

 

Upload the earlier downloaded schema

Next, proceed with the new mapping process as demonstrated below. The newly added fields from the schema will be displayed, allowing them to be mapped within the form layout.

Perform Mapping

After completing the layout, upload it in transaction code SFP. Change the existing custom form and select "Upload Layout". This action will replace the existing layout with the new one.
Save and Activate the Form.

Now create a new report category , for this example exisiting report category is copied . Enter the Report category name and save it designated package .

Create New Report category

Maintain the report activity details by assigning the already developed report definition to this report category. Enter additional information such as description, phase, submission mode, valid form, and validity period.

Add new activity

Save and Activate the report category

Activity details

 

The Custom legal certificate generation involves several steps related to working with Adobe Forms and Report Definitions:

  1. Develop Custom Adobe Form: Design and create a personalized Adobe form tailored to specific needs or requirements.

  2. Create a New Report Definition: Create a new definition for a report, outlining its structure, content, and parameters.

  3. Create Schema for the Document: Develop a schema outlining the structure and format of the document to ensure consistency and compatibility.

  4. Download the Schema: Retrieve the schema for integration purposes.

  5. Perform Mapping In Form: Map data fields or elements within the Adobe form to corresponding elements in the schema or report definition.

  6. Create a New Report Category: Create a new category to classify or organize reports based on specific criteria or characteristics.

  7. Assign Report Definition to the Report Category: Link  the newly created report definition with the designated report category for generation of custom output.

Overall, these steps involve the creation, customization, and organization of Adobe forms and report definitions to effectively manage data and generate custom report within DRC framework.

Summary:

Summary

Thank you for reading!

Drop a like or comment below if you found the above steps useful and do let me know in the comments section if you are looking for any other specific use case of US report extensibility.

entry.title: Re: Unleashing AI and Machine Learning in Sales: Advanced Price-Volume Forecasting with SAP Analytic
entry.content:
Great questions. The focus of this article is more on the capabilities of the product than the business aspects. That said, here are some thoughts I have on your questions: 

About 'Optimized Pricing': Optimizing price planning for maximum revenue involves analyzing various factors such as demand elasticity and competitor pricing. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) provides advanced tools, including predictive modeling and analytics, to help identify optimal price points based on historical data. While SAC can provide insights, achieving maximum revenue requires an understanding of market dynamics and consumer behavior. I believe that with SAC (and BTP) you have all the ingredients to prepare the menu.

Regarding 'reducing manual effort': SAC is designed to streamline processes, and that includes data feeds. Data or prices can come from SAP sources or non-SAP sources and with the capabilities of SAC you can put these numbers into your version and model. It depends a lot on how you tailor your planning. In my example, I copied from actual and plan into my Predictive_ML version (scenario). I can imagine connecting ECC sources as well. I also see great value in using DSP for all data management issues. With DSP and SAC working together, I can see even more sophisticated setups (like federated data) coming.

Thank you for your interest! Let's continue the conversation.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Replication Flow from S/4HANA to Azure Data Lake
entry.content:
@Srikanth Yes, it's the same process works for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and Azure Data Lake as a target. You can create a replication flow and use as a source either CDS Views or local tables with primary keys from SAP Datasphere . Of course you need to load data from CDS Views from S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition to the local tables in case if you'd like to use the local tables.

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: SAP Flexible Workflow Custom Scenario development with My Inbox Integration

Very Nice blog. Thank you for sharing.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Technical Catalog Migration – Why and Getting Ready for Migration
entry.content:
Jocelyn,

Do you recommend creating Business Catalogs in CONF scope or CUST? We only have one-client systems. 

Is there an advantage between CONF and CUST scopes for Business Catalogs, if there is only one client. Even if we had multiple clients, we'd want the same Launchpad configuration in all clients. We are on S/4HANA 2020 FPS02.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

I'm Christopher from South Africa.

I am holder of Bcom in Law and I'm really interested in this initiative.

Regards, 

Christopher

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Scope: What Happens after 2025/2030
entry.content:
As per my understanding the compliance check report only checks whether the IDoc message types are used together with the standard function modules.

Therefore it would be sufficient to simply copy the standard modules to custom modules and continue to use the standard IDoc types (WMTORD, WMCATO, etc).

Do you all agree?

Best regards

Leo.

entry.title: How to create specific entity region cache in SAP Commerce
entry.content:
Introduction

Entity Region cache is a critical region for SAP Commerce performance.  In this article, we will how we can create dedicated region cache for some types to release pressure on Entity Region cache.

 

Why New Cache Region

Entity region cache is capturing by default all types (Items instances). In most case, this strategy is working well when product catalogue is not too large and when user traffic is limited.

However, we can see very high instance eviction when we reach this cache size limit. It is impacting badly performance when eviction is on low volatility data as Product, Category, and Features.

In other hand, high volatility data should be stored in dedicated cache to not pollute default generic region cache (entity region cache). Often, we can identify Cart, CartEntry, PromotionResult, PromotionAction as high volatility data.

By inspecting cache in HAC, we can see easily this behavior.

 

How to create a new Cache Region

Let imagine, we want to create a dedicate cache for Cart and CartEntry type. We need first to get type code. It can be found through BackOffice in Type search

Then, we can declare in spring XML our new cache region

 

<!-- new cache region dedicated for Cart -->
<bean name="cartCacheRegion" class="de.hybris.platform.regioncache.region.impl.EHCacheRegion" lazy-init="true">
<constructor-arg name="name" value="cartCacheRegion" />
<constructor-arg name="maxEntries" value="1000" />
<constructor-arg name="evictionPolicy" value="LRU" />
<constructor-arg name="statsEnabled" value="true" />
<constructor-arg name="exclusiveComputation" value="false" />
<property name="handledTypes">
<array>
<value>43</value> <!-- Cart -->
<value>44</value> <!-- CartEntry -->
</array>
</property>
</bean>

<!-- Add cache region in HAC view -->
<bean id="cartCacheRegionRegistrar" class="de.hybris.platform.regioncache.region.CacheRegionRegistrar" c:region-ref="cartCacheRegion" />

 

This spring XML must be part of global context (project.properties)

 

# Cache regions need to go to the global context
custom.global-context=custom-cache-spring.xml

 

 

After this cache region creation, we see that Cart/CartEntry are not polluting anymore our generic Entity Region cache where low volatility data can stay for long time.

 

Conclusion

Create a new dedicated region cache for some types, it is quite simple and straightforward when we know how to do.

For more advanced use case, you should not hesitate to ask SAP Expert Services help.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Welcome @LeoCortes - great that you are interested in the SAP Community and researching and further learning! I can give you some initial link suggestions below:

  • Products and Technology boards, for example Enterprise Resource Planning
  • Topic pages (get to know about SAP products), for example SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP Managed Tags (subscribe to your topic areas of interest)
  • Join the Career Corner, and see what articles and job opportunities are being posted

Thanks,

Swathi

entry.title: 2024 - Patches/fixes for SAP MII
entry.content:
03/19/2024:

XMII 15.5 SP 000 Patch 36:

  • Conditional | Added Conditional Action Block log entries in transaction debug output, note 3440246
  • History | Improved History Mode for the Scheduler Service, note 3442938
  • Query Number format is not working as expected, note 3443976
  • Adding Transaction Persistence as parameter in case of transaction called via SOAPRunner interface, note 3445523

03/14/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.3 SP 001 Patch 24:

  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.3 SP 001 Patch 53:

  • DB Object is closed error is thrown in case of sql transaction, note 3364376
  • Unable to read DataBufferActionInstance parameter using databuffer service, note 3395994
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • Additional logs for transaction action failure due to timeoutException, note 3407507
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

03/11/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.5 SP 001 Patch 7:

  • System Management - Projects screen assigned roles editing Issue, note 3294426
  • Details header not changing after selecting any tag in simulator services under data services, note 3264633
  • Open activities with checked-out files are still visible after disabling and enabling the NWDI configuration, note 3433017
  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.5 SP 001 Patch 12:

  • Enhancing the logs for MII Start and Stop services, note 3438776
  • Conditional | Added Conditional Action Block log entries in transaction debug output, note 3440246
  • Details header not changing after selecting any tag in simulator services under data services, note 3264633
  • Version history missing in MII workbench, note 3418076
  • Additional logs and traces in message processing module of MII, note 3426125
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • NWDI version history displays limited number of activities, note 3432814
  • Open activities with checked-out files are still visible after disabling and enabling the NWDI configuration, note 3433017
  • DependencyManager | Added dependency check for the MII_ADMIN component version, note 3308632
  • Additional Logging in XMII-Module, note 3340019
  • System Warning window on MII home page for component incompatibility, note 3399037

03/07/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.4 SP 001 Patch 18:

  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.4 SP 001 Patch 45:

  • Conditional | Added Conditional Action Block log entries in transaction debug output, note 3440246
  • DB Object is closed error is thrown in case of sql transaction, note 3364376
  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

03/05/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.5 SP 000 Patch 20:

  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689

XMII 15.5 SP 000 Patch 35:

  • DB Object is closed error is thrown in case of sql transaction, note 3364376
  • Job status shows as scheduled in the Scheduler screen, note 2031689
  • JMS updates causing scheduler breakdown, note 3360558
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • Additional logs for transaction action failure due to timeoutException, note 3407507
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

02/16/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.4 SP 001 Patch 17:

  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267

XMII 15.4 SP 001 Patch 44:

  • Support for X-CSRF-Token in MII HTTP POST action block, note 3429523
  • Usage Status Monitoring for DataServer with wrong date, note 3429629
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • SSO cookie logged and displayed in MII Log Viewer, note 3410041
  • Get Jar Signing related information which gets checked while opening workbench., note 3346027
  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267
  • Scheduler history cleanup default value changed from 24 hours to 72 hours, note 3394218
  • Unable to read DataBufferActionInstance parameter using databuffer service, note 3395994
  • Scheduler history comes blank when job is stuck or not executed in last hour, note 3396099
  • JMS updates causing scheduler breakdown, note 3360558

02/09/2024:

XMII 15.5 SP 001 Patch 11:

  • Running remote workbench with proxy for jar based workbench, note 3369660
  • Unable to read DataBufferActionInstance parameter using databuffer service, note 3395994
  • Source table is missing inside the Global setting on SSCE, note 3424542
  • Settings.view | Event Parameters table headers not visible, note 3426391
  • MII PortalContent.zip file not found under Tools and Archives, note 3427393
  • i5Grid getRowCount() error, note 3428910
  • UIElementTextFieldM | Text Field UI Element size, note 3429413
  • Workbench file export generates the wrong cemd file for SSCE Dashboards, note 3430755
  • Additional logs for transaction action failure due to timeoutException, note 3407507

02/06/2024:

MII_ADMIN 15.3 SP 001 Patch 23:

  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267

XMII 15.3 SP 001 Patch 52:

  • SSO cookie logged and displayed in MII Log Viewer, note 3410041
  • Cannot view changelist details or process a changelist, note 3336846
  • Get Jar Signing related information which gets checked while opening workbench., note 3346027
  • MII workbench login with x.509 certificate based SSO login, note 3370246
  • NWDI configuration gives null pointer exception on save, note 3342267
  • Running remote workbench with proxy for jar based workbench, note 3369660
  • Scheduler history cleanup default value changed from 24 hours to 72 hours, note 3394218
  • New Workbench launcher can not open the workbench if server url has '-' in it, note 3394368
  • Scheduler history comes blank when job is stuck or not executed in last hour, note 3396099
  • JMS updates causing scheduler breakdown, note 3360558

01/17/2024:

XMII 15.4 SP 001 Patch 43:

  • Cannot view changelist details or process a changelist, note 3336846
  • Catalog query with production events is returning Non-PE when there are records in PE table, note 3338596
  • Duplicate Rows inserted in PE table due to duplicate shift rows in MPM_TC37T table, note 3345988
  • PIC Analysis Query - Production events displaying incorrect data, note 3219251
  • Duplicate rows inserted in production table when PE job is run again, note 3333504
  • i5Chart (Gauge Chart Type) is starting from zero value for any change in Value., note 3404399

01/12/2024:

XMII 15.5 SP 001 Patch 10:

  • Catalog query with production events is returning Non-PE when there are records in PE table, note 3338596
  • Additional Logging in XMII-Module, note 3340019
  • MII workbench login with x.509 certificate based SSO login, note 3370246
  • Duplicate Rows inserted in PE table due to duplicate shift rows in MPM_TC37T table, note 3345988
  • Scheduler history cleanup default value changed from 24 hours to 72 hours, note 3394218
  • Production events job creates duplicate records in Production Events table, note 3300655
  • Duplicate rows inserted in production table when PE job is run again, note 3333504
  • i5Chart (Gauge Chart Type) is starting from zero value for any change in Value., note 3404399
  • SSO cookie logged and displayed in MII Log Viewer, note 3410041
  • Bug in SSCE text field i5ValueHelp, note 3413146
  • MII transaction fails with SAP DBTech JDBC: Object is closed error, note 3416130

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: I am Harikrishna Madathala , born in chennai,India, but now am in New Jersey , United States of America for the past 13 years.I work at Microsoft,USA and have 19 years of experience in SAP. I'm driven by curiosity and thrive on challenges, constantly seeking to learn and grow. Creativity is my fuel, and I find joy in exploring new ideas and perspectives. With a passion for innovation, I strive to make a positive impact in all that I do. In every endeavor, I aim to inspire others to embrace their uniqueness and pursue their dreams.

entry.title: Re: Implementer's Guide to Rapid Financial Planning & Analysis for SAP S/4HANA using SAP Analyti
entry.content:
Hi,

i am a little confused these vidoes seem to be about SAC in general. Are there any videos particularly about the Rapid FP&A content?

entry.title: Early Stock Simulation for PMR documents Posting- Consumption & Consumption Reversal
entry.content:
New functionality:

From 1909, SAP has delivered a remarkable feature which allows early S/4 stock check during consumption or consumption reversal posting from EWM related to PMR documents. This early stock simulation for PMR document posting helps in significantly reducing the number of failed queues, improves the scalability and less user intervention to correct the queues.

This functionality can be activated from below configuration.

Extended Warehouse Management-->ERP Integration-->Goods Movements-->Activate Synchronous Goods Movement Posting

F1 help clearly stating the feature, however, functionality is hidden and less explored by customers. 

Replication Step:

I-S/4 and EWM stock not in sync for some reason. ERP stock check.

EWM stock= 415 PC

S/4 Stock= 73 PC

  II - Create PMR & Stage to PSA.

III- Post consumption by Production using Fiori or NWBC

 EWM has already 415 PCS, however S/4 has 73 PCS ONLY

Post consumption:

Stock simulation check does early stock check in S/4 as well during posting from EWM

PMR qty= 300 PCS, S/4 Stock=73, Variance= 300-73=  227 Shortage

This feature helps in reducing stuck queue in S/4 due to stock deficit.

This functionality works for Post Reversal from Production as well in Embedded 1909 onwards. This feature is not supported in Decentralized EWM on S/4 HANA due to system boundary.

This feature is also not supported during Consumption Posting in RF. No early stock check is called from RF framework. If you try to post from RF, EWM allows however, Queue gets stuck in SMQ2 stating deficit of Stock.

Try to post consumption from RF. NO simulation check is called as compared to Fiori.

System allowed to post consumption from RF and there is no S/4 Early stock simulation called as Fiori and its a missing link.

 Thanks for reading. All test have been performed in S/4 HANA Embedded 2022 Sandbox system

entry.title: Re: ATCUD para Portugal: Prepare-se para o novo requisito legal da Portaria 195/2020
entry.content:
Hi Martha,

We have a issue with GLO_PT_SVC. After testing End to End scenarios in Quality system we moved changes to Production. Surprisingly after 1 st Invoice generation the row belonging to series identifier got deleted in GLO_PT_SVC because of which we are getting ATCUD code as 0 in our Invoice. Entries for this Series identifier/invoice type exists in SIPT_NUMBR_SD_V and SIPT_NUMBR_SD_V2 but not in GLO_PT_SVC.

We have already raised incident to SAP. Do you have any idea what may be the cause for deletion of ATCUD code row from  GLO_PT_SVC.

Thanks & Regards,

Divya

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2019.001 v1.62 novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Durante a implementação da SAPnote 3423952 estou tendo o erro:

Include LJ1BBTOP
The field "IDNFBRJ_1BNFPRSMD_CRDT" is unknown, but there is a field
with the similar name "XJ_1BNFPRSMD_CRDT". "XJ_1BNFPRSMD_CRDT".

Contudo, não encontrei a Nota que cria o objeto desconhecido, alguem passou por isso?

Att Eduardo

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Our company has just joined SAS and we are looking forward to interesting articles and information and making contacts for our union store fixture and installation company www.unioninstallers.com.

Our goal is to connect with commercial accounts who would like to learn more about working with the carpenters union crews around the country. Over the years there has been so much misinformation about union workers that I wanted to share experiences we have had with our two decades of union services.

Our company works with retailers, hotels, restaurants, offices and other commercial clients in union areas throughout the USA. I am hoping to share valuable info and insight into union work.
